metadata.title: ColorField
metadata.description: Allow users to select a color with a color picker or as a text input.

properties.description: Allow user to select an alpha value.

A hint as to the intended content of the field. When set to on (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents. When set to off, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes. Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.
The default value for the field.

Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user. This will also be exposed to screen reader users.
Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.
Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.
A unique identifier for the element.
Content to use as the field label.
Changes the visibility of the component's label. visible: the label is visible to all users.exclusive: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.
An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.
A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.
The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the error property.
The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty. The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

examples.name: Basic Usage
examples.description: Standard color input field with hex value.

examples.name: Required
examples.description: Required color field ensuring essential color values are provided.
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.color_field :brand_color, label: "Brand color", value: "#FF0000", required: true %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: With Alpha Transparency
examples.description: Color field supporting alpha channel for transparency control.
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.color_field :background_color, label: "Background color", value: "rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)", alpha: true %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: With Error State
examples.description: Color field with validation error for invalid color format inputs.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-color-field
    label="Brand color"
    name="brandColor"
    value="#invalid"
    error="Please enter a valid color format (hex, rgb, or rgba)"
    required
  ></s-color-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.color_field :brand_color, label: "Brand color", value: "#invalid", error: "Please enter a valid color format (hex, rgb, or rgba)", required: true %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: With Help Text
examples.description: Color field with contextual details providing additional guidance.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-color-field
    label="Primary color"
    value="#1a73e8"
    details="Main brand color used for buttons and links"
  ></s-color-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.color_field :primary_color, label: "Primary color", value: "#1a73e8", details: "Main brand color used for buttons and links" %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: With Placeholder
examples.description: Color field demonstrating how to use a placeholder to guide user input for color selection.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-color-field
    label="Theme color"
    placeholder="Enter a hex color (e.g., #FF0000)"
    value="#000000"
  ></s-color-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.color_field :theme_color, label: "Theme color", placeholder: "Enter a hex color (e.g., #FF0000)", value: "#000000" %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Read Only State
examples.description: Color field in a read-only mode, preventing user modifications to the color value.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-color-field label="System color" name="color" value="#1a73e8" readOnly></s-color-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.color_field :color, label: "System color", value: "#1a73e8", readonly: true %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Form Integration
examples.description: A multi-color field form section demonstrating how ColorField can be used to capture different color settings in a single form.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-section>
    <s-heading>Theme settings</s-heading>
    <s-stack gap="base">
      <s-color-field
        label="Primary brand color"
        name="primaryColor"
        value="#1a73e8"
        defaultValue="#1a73e8"
        details="This color will be used for buttons, links, and brand elements"
        required
      ></s-color-field>
      <s-color-field
        label="Secondary color"
        name="secondaryColor"
        value="#34a853"
        details="Used for secondary actions and accents"
      ></s-color-field>
      <s-color-field
        label="Background overlay"
        name="overlayColor"
        value="rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5)"
        alpha
        details="Background color for modal overlays and dropdowns"
      ></s-color-field>
    </s-stack>
  </s-section>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-section>
    <s-heading>Theme settings</s-heading>
    <s-stack gap="base">
      <%= form.color_field :primary_color, label: "Primary brand color", value: "#1a73e8", defaultvalue: "#1a73e8", details: "This color will be used for buttons, links, and brand elements", required: true %>
      <%= form.color_field :secondary_color, label: "Secondary color", value: "#34a853", details: "Used for secondary actions and accents" %>
      <%= form.color_field :overlay_color, label: "Background overlay", value: "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5)", alpha: true, details: "Background color for modal overlays and dropdowns" %>
    </s-stack>
  </s-section>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Color validation
examples.description: Interactive example showing real-time hex color validation with error messages.
